question_text: Which of the following best describes amplitude?

options.label: A
options.text: The number of waves passing a point each second

options.label: B
options.text: The maximum distance a particle moves from its rest position

options.label: C
options.text: The distance between one wave crest and the next

options.label: D
options.text: The speed at which the wave travels

correct_answer: The maximum distance a particle moves from its rest position
explanation: Amplitude is defined as the maximum distance that a particle in a wave moves away from its rest position (equilibrium). It is measured from the centre line of the wave to either the crest or trough. A larger amplitude means the wave carries more energy.
